avoid: Do not write a custom full d x d GEMM for W @ X; cuBLAS should handle the dominant main term.
Do not explicitly transpose or materialize B.T.
Do not use a fragile U layout such as column-major [16,d] with leading dimension 16.
Do not use SGEMM2 with m=16,n=d for U; use U shape {d,16} with m=d,n=16,k=d,ldc=d.
Do not use opA=T/opB=T in SGEMM1 or SGEMM3 unless the full row-major/column-major mapping is proven correct.
Do not set beta=0 in SGEMM3; the low-rank term must accumulate into the existing Y with beta=1.
Do not hardcode hidden dimensions or assume only one d value.
Do not rely on result caching or repeated identical inputs as an optimization unless the benchmark explicitly defines that workload.
Do not fall back to ATen matmul for normal execution; use direct cuBLAS calls if pursuing the preferred implementation.
evidence: Correctness passes for all tested d values: 3584, 4096, and 4608.
Numerical error is small: max_abs_err=0.00146484375 and rel_l2_err=7.91e-7, within tolerance.
Performance improvement is consistent but modest across shapes: 1.036x at d=3584, 1.029x at d=4096, 1.028x at d=4608.
Aggregate speedup is only 1.031x, suggesting the dominant cost remains the large W @ X SGEMM, while the custom rank-16 path only trims a small amount of overhead.
Profile labels the likely bottleneck as rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth, consistent with a scalar custom rank-16 update being bandwidth-sensitive and limited in upside.
No shape regresses badly, so the implementation is stable across the tested d range, but the gain is too small to conclude that the custom rank-16 kernel is the best direction.
Compile succeeded and static review found no issues, but compile time is high at about 225 seconds.

recommended_actions: try float4 stores when d is divisible by 4
fall back to ATen if numerical error appears
Benchmark a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation before further tuning the handwritten rank-16 update.
Use direct cuBLAS SGEMM for the main W @ X term; do not replace it with a custom full GEMM.
Use the exact qyh-style pure cuBLAS mapping: SGEMM1 opA=N/opB=N with A=X and B=W to produce Y.
For the low-rank intermediate, avoid explicit B.T materialization and compute U with shape {d,16}: SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T with A=X and B=B, m=d, n=16, k=d, ldc=d.
Accumulate the low-rank term directly into Y using SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N with A=U and B=A, m=d, n=d, k=16, beta=1.
If keeping this candidate as a baseline, compare it directly against the pure cuBLAS path on all three shapes because the current speedup is uniform but small.
Only consider vectorized stores or block-size tuning for the handwritten rank-16 update after the pure cuBLAS low-rank SGEMM sequence has been validated and benchmarked.
If future variants show one shape improving while another regresses, add shape-aware dispatch or reject that variant.
